source_quote: The study reports 11 patients lost to follow-up but does not report any deaths. Outcomes measured were oxidative stress markers (MDA, GPx), inflammatory markers (hs-CRP, TNF-alpha, ESR), and lipid profile. No mortality data found in the paper.

outcome: Independent procedure completion: type of endoscopic procedure under study
source_quote: There was also a strong trend toward intubating the cecum more often in the simulator-trained group (26% versus 10%; P=0.06). Table 3: Cecal intubation rate 26% simulator vs 10% control. 10 residents x 5 colonoscopies = 50 procedures in simulator group, 8 x 5 = 40 in control.

outcome: Independent procedure completion: level of participant endoscopy experience
source_quote: Cecal intubation rate was reported overall (26% vs 10%) but not stratified by level of participant endoscopy experience. No subgroup analysis by experience level was presented.

The total insertion time did not differ significantly between the groups because both groups approached the 15 min time limit (14.4+/-0.6 min for simulator group versus 14.6+/-0.5 min for control group; P=0.37).

outcome: Failure to place LMA or ETT at first attempt
source_quote: Table 3: Unsuccessful at first attempt: i-gelSA 3 (8.6%) Yes, 32 (91.4%) No; INSURE 7 (20%) Yes, 28 (80%) No. P=0.55
Table 3: Surfactant reflux: i-gelSA 8 (22.9%) Yes, 27 (77.1%) No; INSURE 6 (17.1%) Yes, 29 (82.9%) No. P=0.17

There was a need for administering the second dose of surfactant in the i-gelSA group for two newborns, while the same need was for three newborns in the INSURE group, but according to the exact Fischer test, it showed no significant difference (P=0.99).
